# Chicago Transit Alerts — full reference

> An unofficial public archive of Chicago Transit Authority (CTA) train and bus plus Metra commuter-rail service alerts and bot-detected disruptions. Not affiliated with the CTA or Metra. This file expands on [/llms.txt](https://chicagotransitalerts.app/llms.txt) with the complete data schema an agent needs to consume the API.

## What this site is

Official alerts and independent bot detections are combined into unified "incidents." An official alert and the bot observation(s) describing the same disruption on the same line within a couple of hours are paired server-side into one incident — no client-side merging. Pairing is within-agency: a CTA alert never merges with a Metra observation, or vice-versa. The site is a static React app on GitHub Pages; the same JSON it renders is published for reuse (research, journalism, dashboards, training data).

Every incident has a `kind` of `train`, `bus`, or `metra`. Train routes use full line names: `red`, `blue`, `green`, `brown`, `orange`, `pink`, `purple`, `yellow`. Bus routes use route numbers/identifiers (`66`, `X9`, `J14`). Metra lines use lowercase route codes: `bnsf`, `hc`, `md-n`, `md-w`, `me`, `ncs`, `ri`, `sws`, `up-n`, `up-nw`, `up-w`. JSON timestamps are epoch milliseconds; CSV timestamps are ISO 8601 (UTC). Metra incidents are present in alerts.json; the CSV and per-line feeds are CTA-only for now.

## Endpoints

- Incidents JSON: https://chicagotransitalerts.app/data/alerts.json
- Daily counts JSON: https://chicagotransitalerts.app/data/daily-counts.json
- Incidents CSV: https://chicagotransitalerts.app/data/alerts.csv
- Data changelog: https://chicagotransitalerts.app/data/CHANGELOG.md
- Atom feed: https://chicagotransitalerts.app/feed.xml (per-line/route: /feed/line/<line>.xml, /feed/route/<route>.xml)
- JSON feed: https://chicagotransitalerts.app/feed.json
- API catalog (RFC 9264 linkset): https://chicagotransitalerts.app/.well-known/api-catalog

Regenerated whenever the underlying data changes (typically every ~7 minutes when there's activity).

## alerts.json shape

The top-level array is `incidents` — one object per real-world disruption.

```jsonc
{
  "generated_at": 1715200000000,        // epoch ms when the snapshot was produced
  "data_start_ts": 1707350400000,       // earliest moment with coverage
  "incidents": [
    {
      "id": "3k2j...",                  // stable permalink id (Bluesky post rkey); /event/:id
      "kind": "train",                  // "train", "bus", or "metra"
      "routes": ["red"],                // full line names, bus route numbers, or Metra codes (e.g. "up-n")
      "first_seen_ts": 1715199000000,
      "resolved_ts": null,              // null = still open
      "active": true,
      "sources": ["cta", "bot"],        // contributors: "cta", "bot", or both
      "cta": {                          // null for bot-only incidents
        "alert_id": "...",
        "headline": "...",
        "short_description": "...",     // CTA body text (reroute/closure details)
        "post_url": "https://bsky.app/profile/.../post/...",
        "resolved_reply_url": null,
        "first_seen_ts": 1715199000000, // CTA's own lifecycle, distinct from the incident's
        "resolved_ts": null,
        "active": true,
        "affected_from_station": null,
        "affected_to_station": null,
        "affected_direction": null,
        "mentioned_stations": [],       // canonical station names parsed from the alert
        "affected_stations": [],        // full segment fill (endpoints + inner stops); [] when none
        "cta_event_start_ts": null,
        "cta_event_end_ts": null,
        "cancellation": null            // Metra single-train annulment (else null): { state:
                                        // 'upcoming'|'cancelled', scheduled_departure_ts,
                                        // scheduled_arrival_ts, train_number, origin }
        // "versions": [...]            // present only when CTA edited the alert over time
      },
      "observations": [                 // [] for CTA-only incidents
        {
          "id": 12345,
          "detection_source": "pulse-cold", // see glossary below
          "signals": ["gap", "bunching"],   // populated for roundups
          "from_station": "Howard",
          "to_station": "Loyola",
          "stations": ["Howard", "Jarvis", "Morse", "Loyola"], // full stretch (endpoints + inner
                                        // stops), from→to; omitted when not enumerable
          "direction_label": "toward the Loop", // null when unavailable
          "ts": 1715199000000,          // when the bot posted; matches post_url
          "onset_ts": 1715197860000,    // disruption start, back-dated to last observed train
                                        // (pulse-cold/thin-gap); null when not back-dated — use ts
          "resolved_ts": 1715202600000,
          "duration_ms": 4740000,       // resolved_ts - (onset_ts ?? ts); null while active
          "active": false,
          "post_url": "https://bsky.app/profile/.../post/...",
          "resolved_post_url": null,
          "bot_description": "…",       // pre-rendered plain-English summary
          "onset_description": "…",     // pre-rendered onset-timeline sentence; omitted when no back-date
          "evidence": { /* small "why the bot fired" payload */ }
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

## alerts.csv columns

One row per alert or observation (incidents flattened), with an explicit `type` column:

```
type, id, kind, routes, headline, detection_source, signals, from_station, to_station,
direction, direction_label, first_seen_ts, onset_ts, resolved_ts, duration_minutes, active,
post_url, resolved_post_url
```

Timestamps are ISO 8601 (UTC). `routes` (full line names) and `signals` are semicolon-separated when multi-valued. `onset_ts` is the disruption start for absence-style observations (back-dated from `first_seen_ts` to the last observed train) and is blank when not back-dated; `duration_minutes` is measured from `onset_ts` when present, else `first_seen_ts`.

## detection_source glossary

- `gap` — a longer-than-expected gap between trains/buses on a route+direction.
- `bunching` — vehicles arriving clustered together after a gap.
- `ghost` — scheduled service that never materialized (ghost trains/buses).
- `pulse-held` — a vehicle held stationary mid-route.
- `pulse-cold` — a platform/segment that went "cold" (no service observed).
- `thin-gap` — a smaller gap flagged with back-dated onset.
- `roundup` — multiple simultaneous signals summarized into one observation (`signals[]` populated).
- `cancellation` — a Metra train Metra confirmed as cancelled (the timetabled analog of `ghost`).
- `cancellation-inferred` — a scheduled Metra train the bot never saw run, with no covering alert (hedged; suppressed during fleet-wide feed outages).
- `delay` — a Metra train running materially behind schedule (15+ min).

## Etiquette

Public data, no auth. Please cache responses, don't poll faster than every few minutes, and credit the project if you build something public. Breaking changes to the shape are recorded in the data changelog — check it before pinning. Source and field-by-field JSDoc: https://github.com/cailinpitt/chicago-transit-alerts
